candidate.name: multi-thread block_on drives shared IO driver while waiting
candidate.hypothesis: For canonical concurrency=1, ylong_runtime::block_on waits on a condvar while global runtime workers drive IO. Letting block_on directly drive the shared IO driver could reduce wake-to-poll latency for HTTPS proxy response reads.

red.result: failed as expected before implementation: unresolved import block_on_with_driver

green.result: passed after implementation before benchmark rejection

focused_benchmark.command: source ~/.cargo/env && python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model threaded --scenario all --requests 1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 1 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_s9d_blockon_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-runtime-blockon-driver-focused --figure-dir docs/figures/ylong-runtime-blockon-driver-focused

phase_diagnostic.command: source ~/.cargo/env && python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model threaded --scenario all --requests 1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 1 --phase-timing --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_s9d_blockon_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-runtime-blockon-driver-phase --figure-dir docs/figures/ylong-runtime-blockon-driver-phase
phase_diagnostic.finding: Phase run remained mixed and did not overturn the non-phase focused counterexample. HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy still failed the SOTA entry bar, and HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy phase throughput was below libcurl.

decision.reason: The candidate slightly helped HTTPS-origin proxy cells in the non-phase focused run, but it severely regressed HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy throughput, p50, p95, and CPU. It fails every-cell throughput, p50, p95, CPU geomean, and flagship gates, so it must not enter full S9D or S10.
